biography.profession: Night Manager / Repeater / Pretender to the Crown
biography.personalDetails:
Aliases: Night Manager (of the Macallistar Building / Hotel Broadalbin), Mr. Castaigne (used by tenants), J.C. (Thomas Manuel's childhood name for him)

Role: Night Manager / Repeater / Pretender to the Crown

Nature: unnatural

Location: loc_castaigne_apartment

Player Description

An old man in clothes that look Victorian answers the door — impossibly old-fashioned, right down to his slippers. He has white unkempt hair and walks with a bad limp. He introduces himself as the building's night manager, a Mister Castaigne. His English is excellent but faintly accented. He has a kind smile that somehow feels like it was assembled by committee.

Handler Notes

STUCK: repeats patterns (considers the Imperial Dynasty, drinks, wanders the Night Floors performing small chores). ENDLESS DOORS: can always find exits via hidden passages, secret doors, or new doors; others may follow gaining +1 Corruption. INFINITE: human-fragile but returns unharmed after being killed or injured; still recalls who harmed him. WHERE'S MY BOTTLE?: obsessed with his Soul Bottle in the Whisper Labyrinth; his frailty and fear of rivals prevents him from undertaking the expedition. His locked bedroom contains The Imperial Dynasty of America, a bundle of ragged worn handwritten pages tracking a complex lineage — Castaigne's own name is inside. If discovered, he explodes into murderous frenzy crying 'No! No! The crown is mine! Mine!' Once restrained he only pouts and mumbles in an unknown language. His old newspapers/magazines (none newer than 1940) include headlines pointing to Carcosa's nature: e.g., 'Russo-Germanic Pact Crumbles, Vienna Liberated' (New York Tribune, 1 JUL 1923). The sheer volume and apparent age of these papers cost 0/1 SAN from the unnatural. EXEUNT note: The 1947 family photo in basement storage shows Lundine family with a large mastiff captioned 'Family w Abraham, Christmas 47' — seeing this costs 0/1 SAN from the unnatural. The Superintendent's party occurs every night — Castaigne says 'the Super lives upstairs and is having a party tonight.' He can lead 0-SAN Agents to this fatal encounter. Native language Tartessian 70% — he is the only character who speaks it as a native tongue. Motivations: To become King of America. To enlist aid to destroy cousin Hildred Castaigne. To kill all other pretenders to the throne. To locate his Soul Bottle.

Sanity Effects

Trigger: Discovering The Imperial Dynasty of America in Castaigne's locked bedroom

SAN Loss: 0/0

No direct SAN loss from reading the document, but Castaigne's murderous rage response to discovery is the threat. Seeing one's own name in his lineage rolls costs 0/1 SAN from the unnatural and grants +1 Corruption.

Relationships

  • Cousin / Rival: npc_hildred_castaigne — Hildred Castaigne is another resident of the Night World. The two have met on occasion. Each bitterly disputes the other's claim to the crown of America. Henri considers Hildred a rival pretender to be destroyed.
  • Night World Tenant: npc_thomas_manuel — Manuel calls him 'Mister Castaigne' and refers to him as the building's night manager. Castaigne lets Manuel work in the basement. Manuel knew him as childhood imaginary friend 'J.C.'
  • Night World Associate: npc_mark_roark — Roark knows him as the night manager of Hotel Broadalbin. Describes him as 'a dago.'
  • Ancestor: npc_gabriel_castaigne — Gabriel Castaigne (clockmaker, 15th century) appears to be an ancestor; the 'G. CASTAIGNE' maker's mark on the Like Clockwork manifestation's automaton pieces, dated FR. 1435, connects to him.
  • Ancestor: npc_augustus_chastaigne — Augustus Chastaigne (1402-1403) appears to be the earliest known ancestor; A Horse By Degrees is signed 'A. Chastaigne, G. Castaigne, H. Castaigne.'
